trigger: after opening_powerhouse_goal and before first free-play objective

stops.title: GARAGE BAY · MATERIAL TO SHIPMENT
stops.location: East wall mission board · full route overview
stops.operation: Follow the pulsing objective and use GO TO & OPEN when you need guidance.
stops.text: Before we take the first order, learn the floor. Material enters at receiving, moves through preparation and machining, then leaves only after inspection and a clean handoff.

stops.title: PLANNING DESK · ACCEPT AND ROUTE WORK
stops.location: North wall · left of the NOX terminal
stops.operation: Open the desk to accept the next customer job and read its traveler.
stops.text: This is the planning desk. Open it to accept customer work, read the print, confirm the material, and understand the route before anyone touches the stock.

stops.title: NOX TERMINAL · ORDER CERTIFIED STOCK
stops.location: North wall · immediately right of planning
stops.operation: Choose the required alloy and dimensions, verify coin cost, and confirm the order.
stops.text: The NOX terminal is where you order certified raw material. Match the alloy and size to the job, check the cost, then confirm delivery to receiving.

stops.title: BANDSAW · PREPARE THE RAW BLANK
stops.location: West production lane · between receiving and the manual mill
stops.operation: Accept a job first, open the saw, and enter the cut length shown on the traveler.
stops.text: The bandsaw turns delivered stock into a controlled blank. Open the station, enter the required cut length, and leave enough material for clean finishing operations.

stops.title: TOOL CART · BUILD THE CNC TOOL KIT
stops.operation: Select the cutter, set stickout, add the probe and chamfer tool, then install all three.
stops.text: The tool cart is your setup station. Choose the correct cutter, control stickout, gauge the probe and chamfer tool, and install the complete kit before machining.

stops.title: VMC · PROVE THE PROGRAM AND MAKE THE PART
stops.location: Center machine island · right of the tool cart
stops.operation: Prepare stock and tools first, open the VMC, complete the G-code checks, and start the cycle.
stops.text: The vertical machining center makes the part. Load only prepared stock and a complete tool kit, verify every G code answer, then run and inspect the result.

stops.title: SHOP CLASS · LEARN THE WHY
stops.operation: Approach the chalkboard to review manufacturing principles and formulas.
stops.text: Shop Class is where we stop and learn the reason behind the process. Use it to review speeds, feeds, material behavior, quality, and the math that protects the machine.

stops.title: MANUAL MILL · DIRECT MACHINE CONTROL
stops.location: West production lane · below the bandsaw
stops.operation: Orientation only: the production minigame remains a documented release gap.
stops.text: The manual mill teaches direct control of workholding, spindle speed, feed, and depth. Its production lesson is orientation only until the manual machining task is enabled.

stops.title: DEBURR BENCH · SAFE FINISHED EDGES
stops.operation: Orientation only: deburr interaction and edge-quality scoring remain to be implemented.
stops.text: The deburr bench removes sharp edges and prepares the part for inspection. The future task will require the correct edge break without damaging a finished feature.

stops.title: CNC LATHE · TURNING WORK
stops.location: East machine island · right of the VMC
stops.operation: Open the lathe for an equipment inspection; production turning remains a future workflow.
stops.text: The CNC lathe rotates the stock against the tool. Inspect chuck pressure, stock stickout, turret clearance, and the program before any production cycle.

stops.title: MTCONNECT · MACHINE VISIBILITY
stops.operation: Orientation only: connectivity gameplay unlocks in a later facility chapter.
stops.text: The MTConnect node exposes machine status and production signals. It becomes useful when the shop grows from watching one machine to managing an entire connected system.

stops.title: JOBLINE · SHIFT HANDOFF
stops.operation: Orientation only: handoff gameplay unlocks with multi-person production.
stops.text: The JobLine handoff terminal records what ran, what comes next, and what looks unusual. A clean handoff prevents the next shift from starting with missing context.

stops.title: RECEIVING · VERIFY INCOMING STOCK
stops.operation: After ordering, verify the incoming alloy, dimensions, quantity, and certification.
stops.text: Receiving is where certified stock enters the shop. Verify the alloy, dimensions, quantity, and certification before material moves into the production route.

stops.title: MISSION BOARD · KNOW THE NEXT STEP
stops.operation: Read the objective, requirements, job count, and quality gate; use GO TO & OPEN for routing.
stops.text: The mission board shows the current objective and chapter gate. When you are unsure, follow the pulsing target or use Go To and Open to reach the correct station.
